query:
INSERT INTO quiz_questions (lessonId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, questionOrder) VALUES
-- GED-LANG Lesson 9: Essay Writing Fundamentals (540001)
(540001, 'A thesis statement should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Be vague","Clearly state the main argument","Appear at the end","Be a question"]', 'Clearly state the main argument', 1),
(540001, 'The introduction paragraph should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Summarize everything","Hook the reader and present the thesis","Be the longest paragraph","Avoid the topic"]', 'Hook the reader and present the thesis', 2),
(540001, 'Body paragraphs should each focus on:', 'multiple_choice', '["Multiple topics","One main idea with supporting evidence","The conclusion","Personal opinions only"]', 'One main idea with supporting evidence', 3),
(540001, 'Transitions between paragraphs help:', 'multiple_choice', '["Confuse readers","Create flow and logical connections","Add length","Repeat ideas"]', 'Create flow and logical connections', 4),
(540001, 'The conclusion should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Introduce new ideas","Summarize and reinforce the thesis","Be identical to the introduction","Be optional"]', 'Summarize and reinforce the thesis', 5),
(540001, 'Evidence in essays should be:', 'multiple_choice', '["Made up","Specific, relevant, and properly cited","Vague","Avoided"]', 'Specific, relevant, and properly cited', 6),
(540001, 'Topic sentences:', 'multiple_choice', '["Are unnecessary","Introduce the main idea of each paragraph","Should be hidden","Go at the end"]', 'Introduce the main idea of each paragraph', 7),
(540001, 'Formal essay writing avoids:', 'multiple_choice', '["Clear language","Slang and contractions","Evidence","Structure"]', 'Slang and contractions', 8),
(540001, 'Proofreading involves checking for:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","Grammar, spelling, and punctuation errors","Only length","Ideas only"]', 'Grammar, spelling, and punctuation errors', 9),
(540001, 'A well-organized essay follows:', 'multiple_choice', '["No pattern","A logical structure with clear progression","Random order","Stream of consciousness"]', 'A logical structure with clear progression', 10),
(540001, 'Explain the key components of a well-structured essay.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'A well-structured essay includes an introduction with thesis, body paragraphs with evidence, and a conclusion that reinforces the main argument.', 11),

-- GED-LANG Lesson 10: Argumentative Writing (540002)
(540002, 'Argumentative writing aims to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Entertain","Persuade the reader to accept a position","Describe events","Tell a story"]', 'Persuade the reader to accept a position', 1),
(540002, 'A claim in argumentative writing is:', 'multiple_choice', '["A fact","The main argument or position","A question","A summary"]', 'The main argument or position', 2),
(540002, 'Counterarguments should be:', 'multiple_choice', '["Ignored","Acknowledged and refuted","Agreed with entirely","Avoided"]', 'Acknowledged and refuted', 3),
(540002, 'Logical fallacies are:', 'multiple_choice', '["Strong arguments","Errors in reasoning that weaken arguments","Required","Helpful"]', 'Errors in reasoning that weaken arguments', 4),
(540002, 'Ethos appeals to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Emotion","Credibility and ethics","Logic","Fear"]', 'Credibility and ethics', 5),
(540002, 'Pathos appeals to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Logic","Emotions","Authority","Statistics"]', 'Emotions', 6),
(540002, 'Logos appeals to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Emotions","Logic and reason","Personal stories","Credibility"]', 'Logic and reason', 7),
(540002, 'Strong evidence includes:', 'multiple_choice', '["Personal opinions only","Facts, statistics, and expert testimony","Assumptions","Generalizations"]', 'Facts, statistics, and expert testimony', 8),
(540002, 'The call to action in argumentative writing:', 'multiple_choice', '["Is unnecessary","Tells readers what to do or think","Should be vague","Comes first"]', 'Tells readers what to do or think', 9),
(540002, 'Bias in sources should be:', 'multiple_choice', '["Ignored","Identified and evaluated","Preferred","Hidden"]', 'Identified and evaluated', 10),
(540002, 'Describe the elements of effective argumentative writing.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Effective argumentative writing includes a clear claim, strong evidence, acknowledgment of counterarguments, and logical reasoning.', 11),

-- GED-LANG Lesson 11: Reading Comprehension Strategies (540003)
(540003, 'Active reading involves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Passive scanning","Engaging with the text through questioning and annotation","Speed reading only","Memorization"]', 'Engaging with the text through questioning and annotation', 1),
(540003, 'The main idea of a passage is:', 'multiple_choice', '["A minor detail","The central point the author is making","The first sentence always","A supporting fact"]', 'The central point the author is making', 2),
(540003, 'Context clues help readers:', 'multiple_choice', '["Skip words","Determine meaning of unfamiliar words","Ignore vocabulary","Read faster"]', 'Determine meaning of unfamiliar words', 3),
(540003, 'Making inferences means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Guessing randomly","Drawing conclusions from evidence in the text","Copying the text","Ignoring details"]', 'Drawing conclusions from evidence in the text', 4),
(540003, 'Summarizing a passage requires:', 'multiple_choice', '["Copying it exactly","Identifying and restating key points briefly","Adding new information","Ignoring the main idea"]', 'Identifying and restating key points briefly', 5),
(540003, 'Authors purpose can be to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only entertain","Inform, persuade, or entertain","Confuse","None of the above"]', 'Inform, persuade, or entertain', 6),
(540003, 'Tone in writing refers to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Volume","The authors attitude toward the subject","Font size","Length"]', 'The authors attitude toward the subject', 7),
(540003, 'Comparing texts involves finding:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only differences","Similarities and differences","Nothing useful","Errors"]', 'Similarities and differences', 8),
(540003, 'Previewing a text before reading helps:', 'multiple_choice', '["Waste time","Activate prior knowledge and set purpose","Skip reading","Avoid comprehension"]', 'Activate prior knowledge and set purpose', 9),
(540003, 'Asking questions while reading:', 'multiple_choice', '["Slows comprehension","Improves understanding and engagement","Is unnecessary","Should be avoided"]', 'Improves understanding and engagement', 10),
(540003, 'Explain effective strategies for improving reading comprehension.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Effective strategies include previewing, active reading, questioning, making inferences, and summarizing key points.', 11),

-- GED-LANG Lesson 12: Grammar and Sentence Structure (540004)
(540004, 'A complete sentence must have:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only a verb","A subject and a predicate","Only a subject","No punctuation"]', 'A subject and a predicate', 1),
(540004, 'A run-on sentence:', 'multiple_choice', '["Is too short","Joins independent clauses incorrectly","Is always correct","Has no subject"]', 'Joins independent clauses incorrectly', 2),
(540004, 'Subject-verb agreement means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Subjects and verbs can differ","Subjects and verbs must match in number","Verbs are optional","Subjects are optional"]', 'Subjects and verbs must match in number', 3),
(540004, 'A comma splice is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Correct punctuation","Incorrectly joining sentences with only a comma","A type of sentence","Required"]', 'Incorrectly joining sentences with only a comma', 4),
(540004, 'Parallel structure means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random patterns","Using consistent grammatical forms in a series","Mixing forms","Avoiding patterns"]', 'Using consistent grammatical forms in a series', 5),
(540004, 'Pronouns must agree with their:', 'multiple_choice', '["Verbs","Antecedents in number and gender","Adjectives","Nothing"]', 'Antecedents in number and gender', 6),
(540004, 'A fragment is:', 'multiple_choice', '["A complete sentence","An incomplete sentence","A paragraph","A run-on"]', 'An incomplete sentence', 7),
(540004, 'Modifiers should be placed:', 'multiple_choice', '["Anywhere","Near the words they modify","At the end always","At the beginning always"]', 'Near the words they modify', 8),
(540004, 'Active voice is generally preferred because it is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Weaker","More direct and clear","Passive","Confusing"]', 'More direct and clear', 9),
(540004, 'Semicolons are used to:', 'multiple_choice', '["End sentences","Join related independent clauses","Replace periods always","Introduce lists"]', 'Join related independent clauses', 10),
(540004, 'Explain common grammar errors and how to correct them.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Common errors include run-ons, fragments, subject-verb disagreement, and comma splices, corrected by proper punctuation and structure.', 11),

-- GED-LANG Lesson 13: Vocabulary Development (540005)
(540005, 'Root words help readers:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","Understand word meanings and build vocabulary","Confuse words","Avoid learning"]', 'Understand word meanings and build vocabulary', 1),
(540005, 'Prefixes are added to:', 'multiple_choice', '["The end of words","The beginning of words","The middle","Nowhere"]', 'The beginning of words', 2),
(540005, 'Suffixes change a words:', 'multiple_choice', '["Spelling only","Meaning or part of speech","Nothing","Pronunciation only"]', 'Meaning or part of speech', 3),
(540005, 'The prefix un- means:', 'multiple_choice', '["More","Not or opposite","Again","Before"]', 'Not or opposite', 4),
(540005, 'Synonyms are words that:', 'multiple_choice', '["Mean the opposite","Have similar meanings","Sound alike","Are spelled alike"]', 'Have similar meanings', 5),
(540005, 'Antonyms are words that:', 'multiple_choice', '["Mean the same","Have opposite meanings","Sound alike","Are spelled alike"]', 'Have opposite meanings', 6),
(540005, 'Context clues include:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing helpful","Definitions, examples, and surrounding words","Random information","Pictures only"]', 'Definitions, examples, and surrounding words', 7),
(540005, 'Denotation refers to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Emotional meaning","Dictionary definition","Personal interpretation","Slang meaning"]', 'Dictionary definition', 8),
(540005, 'Connotation refers to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Dictionary definition","Emotional or cultural associations","Spelling","Pronunciation"]', 'Emotional or cultural associations', 9),
(540005, 'Building vocabulary requires:', 'multiple_choice', '["No effort","Regular reading and practice","Avoiding new words","Memorization only"]', 'Regular reading and practice', 10),
(540005, 'Describe strategies for building vocabulary effectively.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Effective strategies include learning roots, prefixes, suffixes, using context clues, and regular reading practice.', 11),

-- GED-LANG Lesson 14: Analyzing Literary Texts (540006)
(540006, 'Theme in literature is:', 'multiple_choice', '["The plot","The underlying message or central idea","The setting","The characters"]', 'The underlying message or central idea', 1),
(540006, 'Characterization reveals:', 'multiple_choice', '["Setting only","Personality traits and motivations of characters","Plot only","Theme only"]', 'Personality traits and motivations of characters', 2),
(540006, 'Symbolism uses objects to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Decorate","Represent abstract ideas or concepts","Confuse readers","Fill space"]', 'Represent abstract ideas or concepts', 3),
(540006, 'Foreshadowing provides:', 'multiple_choice', '["Confusion","Hints about future events","Flashbacks","Character descriptions"]', 'Hints about future events', 4),
(540006, 'Point of view affects:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","How the story is told and what readers know","Only setting","Only theme"]', 'How the story is told and what readers know', 5),
(540006, 'First-person narration uses:', 'multiple_choice', '["He/she","I/we","They","You"]', 'I/we', 6),
(540006, 'Conflict in literature can be:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only external","Internal or external","Neither","Avoided"]', 'Internal or external', 7),
(540006, 'Imagery appeals to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Logic only","The five senses","Nothing","Statistics"]', 'The five senses', 8),
(540006, 'Irony occurs when:', 'multiple_choice', '["Things are as expected","There is a contrast between expectation and reality","Nothing happens","Characters agree"]', 'There is a contrast between expectation and reality', 9),
(540006, 'Analyzing literature requires:', 'multiple_choice', '["Surface reading only","Close reading and critical thinking","Skimming","Memorization"]', 'Close reading and critical thinking', 10),
(540006, 'Explain key literary elements and how to analyze them in texts.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Key elements include theme, characterization, symbolism, point of view, and conflict, analyzed through close reading.', 11),

-- GED-LANG Lesson 15: Analyzing Informational Texts (540007)
(540007, 'Informational texts aim to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Entertain only","Inform, explain, or persuade","Tell fictional stories","Avoid facts"]', 'Inform, explain, or persuade', 1),
(540007, 'The central idea is:', 'multiple_choice', '["A minor detail","The main point of the text","The authors name","The title"]', 'The main point of the text', 2),
(540007, 'Supporting details:', 'multiple_choice', '["Contradict the main idea","Provide evidence for the central idea","Are unnecessary","Should be ignored"]', 'Provide evidence for the central idea', 3),
(540007, 'Text structure includes:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random organization","Cause/effect, compare/contrast, sequence","No pattern","Only chronological"]', 'Cause/effect, compare/contrast, sequence', 4),
(540007, 'Evaluating sources requires checking:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","Credibility, bias, and accuracy","Only length","Only date"]', 'Credibility, bias, and accuracy', 5),
(540007, 'Facts can be:', 'multiple_choice', '["Never verified","Proven true or false","Only opinions","Ignored"]', 'Proven true or false', 6),
(540007, 'Opinions express:', 'multiple_choice', '["Verifiable facts","Personal beliefs or judgments","Universal truths","Nothing"]', 'Personal beliefs or judgments', 7),
(540007, 'Authors bias may:', 'multiple_choice', '["Not exist","Influence how information is presented","Be obvious always","Be helpful always"]', 'Influence how information is presented', 8),
(540007, 'Synthesizing information means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Copying","Combining information from multiple sources","Ignoring sources","Summarizing one source"]', 'Combining information from multiple sources', 9),
(540007, 'Critical reading involves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Accepting everything","Questioning and evaluating the text","Speed reading","Skimming"]', 'Questioning and evaluating the text', 10),
(540007, 'Describe how to analyze and evaluate informational texts effectively.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Effective analysis includes identifying central ideas, evaluating evidence, recognizing bias, and synthesizing information.', 11),

-- GED-LANG Lesson 16: Extended Response Writing (540008)
(540008, 'The GED extended response requires:', 'multiple_choice', '["A creative story","An evidence-based argument analyzing source texts","A personal narrative","A summary only"]', 'An evidence-based argument analyzing source texts', 1),
(540008, 'Time management for the extended response should include:', 'multiple_choice', '["Writing immediately","Planning, writing, and revising time","Only writing","Only planning"]', 'Planning, writing, and revising time', 2),
(540008, 'Evidence from source texts should be:', 'multiple_choice', '["Ignored","Quoted or paraphrased with analysis","Made up","Copied entirely"]', 'Quoted or paraphrased with analysis', 3),
(540008, 'The response should analyze:', 'multiple_choice', '["Personal experiences only","How well the author supports their argument","Random topics","Unrelated texts"]', 'How well the author supports their argument', 4),
(540008, 'A strong thesis for the extended response:', 'multiple_choice', '["Is vague","Takes a clear position on the effectiveness of the argument","Avoids the topic","Is a question"]', 'Takes a clear position on the effectiveness of the argument', 5),
(540008, 'Body paragraphs should include:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only opinions","Topic sentences, evidence, and analysis","Random thoughts","No structure"]', 'Topic sentences, evidence, and analysis', 6),
(540008, 'Transitions help:', 'multiple_choice', '["Confuse readers","Connect ideas and improve flow","Add length only","Nothing"]', 'Connect ideas and improve flow', 7),
(540008, 'The conclusion should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Introduce new ideas","Reinforce the thesis and summarize key points","Be skipped","Repeat the introduction exactly"]', 'Reinforce the thesis and summarize key points', 8),
(540008, 'Revision should check for:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","Clarity, organization, and grammar","Length only","Spelling only"]', 'Clarity, organization, and grammar', 9),
(540008, 'Scoring criteria include:', 'multiple_choice', '["Length only","Analysis, organization, language use, and conventions","Handwriting","Speed"]', 'Analysis, organization, language use, and conventions', 10),
(540008, 'Explain the key components of a successful GED extended response.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'A successful response includes a clear thesis, evidence-based analysis, logical organization, and proper conventions.', 11),

-- GED-LANG Lesson 17: Test-Taking Strategies (540009)
(540009, 'Before the test, you should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Stay up all night","Get adequate rest and prepare materials","Skip breakfast","Arrive late"]', 'Get adequate rest and prepare materials', 1),
(540009, 'Reading questions carefully helps:', 'multiple_choice', '["Waste time","Understand what is being asked","Nothing","Confuse you"]', 'Understand what is being asked', 2),
(540009, 'Process of elimination involves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Guessing randomly","Removing obviously wrong answers","Choosing the first answer","Skipping questions"]', 'Removing obviously wrong answers', 3),
(540009, 'Time management during the test means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Spending all time on one question","Pacing yourself through all sections","Rushing through everything","Ignoring time"]', 'Pacing yourself through all sections', 4),
(540009, 'If unsure of an answer:', 'multiple_choice', '["Leave it blank","Make an educated guess and move on","Spend unlimited time","Give up"]', 'Make an educated guess and move on', 5),
(540009, 'Reviewing answers is important to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Waste time","Catch and correct errors","Change everything","Nothing"]', 'Catch and correct errors', 6),
(540009, 'Test anxiety can be managed by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Ignoring it","Deep breathing and positive self-talk","Panicking","Leaving the test"]', 'Deep breathing and positive self-talk', 7),
(540009, 'Key words in questions like NOT or EXCEPT indicate:', 'multiple_choice', '["Normal questions","Looking for the opposite or exception","Trick questions","Easy questions"]', 'Looking for the opposite or exception', 8),
(540009, 'For reading passages, you should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Skip the passage","Read actively and refer back for answers","Memorize everything","Guess without reading"]', 'Read actively and refer back for answers', 9),
(540009, 'Staying calm during the test helps:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","Maintain focus and think clearly","Waste time","Increase anxiety"]', 'Maintain focus and think clearly', 10),
(540009, 'Describe effective test-taking strategies for the GED Language Arts test.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Effective strategies include preparation, time management, process of elimination, careful reading, and managing test anxiety.', 11),

-- GED-LANG Lesson 18: Practice and Review (540010)
(540010, 'Regular practice helps:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","Build skills and confidence","Increase anxiety","Waste time"]', 'Build skills and confidence', 1),
(540010, 'Reviewing mistakes helps:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","Identify areas for improvement","Feel bad","Repeat errors"]', 'Identify areas for improvement', 2),
(540010, 'Practice tests simulate:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing useful","Actual test conditions and format","Easy questions only","Irrelevant content"]', 'Actual test conditions and format', 3),
(540010, 'Weak areas should be:', 'multiple_choice', '["Ignored","Given extra study time and practice","Avoided","Accepted"]', 'Given extra study time and practice', 4),
(540010, 'Study schedules help:', 'multiple_choice', '["Waste time","Organize and prioritize learning","Nothing","Increase stress"]', 'Organize and prioritize learning', 5),
(540010, 'Active recall involves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Passive reading","Testing yourself on material","Highlighting only","Copying notes"]', 'Testing yourself on material', 6),
(540010, 'Spaced repetition means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Cramming","Reviewing material at increasing intervals","Studying once","Forgetting"]', 'Reviewing material at increasing intervals', 7),
(540010, 'Study groups can help:', 'multiple_choice', '["Distract","Share knowledge and stay motivated","Waste time","Nothing"]', 'Share knowledge and stay motivated', 8),
(540010, 'Using multiple resources:', 'multiple_choice', '["Is confusing","Provides different perspectives and reinforcement","Is unnecessary","Should be avoided"]', 'Provides different perspectives and reinforcement', 9),
(540010, 'Confidence comes from:', 'multiple_choice', '["Luck","Preparation and practice","Avoiding study","Guessing"]', 'Preparation and practice', 10),
(540010, 'Explain effective study and review strategies for GED preparation.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Effective strategies include regular practice, reviewing mistakes, using practice tests, and focusing on weak areas.', 11);
command:
mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway02.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user 2KiCrCqNDFcXt7P.577d1a63eeb5 --database P2Dpw9MQsmxzdUQCfGjV6b --execute INSERT INTO quiz_questions (lessonId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, questionOrder) VALUES
-- GED-LANG Lesson 9: Essay Writing Fundamentals (540001)
(540001, 'A thesis statement should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Be vague","Clearly state the main argument","Appear at the end","Be a question"]', 'Clearly state the main argument', 1),
(540001, 'The introduction paragraph should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Summarize everything","Hook the reader and present the thesis","Be the longest paragraph","Avoid the topic"]', 'Hook the reader and present the thesis', 2),
(540001, 'Body paragraphs should each focus on:', 'multiple_choice', '["Multiple topics","One main idea with supporting evidence","The conclusion","Personal opinions only"]', 'One main idea with supporting evidence', 3),
(540001, 'Transitions between paragraphs help:', 'multiple_choice', '["Confuse readers","Create flow and logical connections","Add length","Repeat ideas"]', 'Create flow and logical connections', 4),
(540001, 'The conclusion should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Introduce new ideas","Summarize and reinforce the thesis","Be identical to the introduction","Be optional"]', 'Summarize and reinforce the thesis', 5),
(540001, 'Evidence in essays should be:', 'multiple_choice', '["Made up","Specific, relevant, and properly cited","Vague","Avoided"]', 'Specific, relevant, and properly cited', 6),
(540001, 'Topic sentences:', 'multiple_choice', '["Are unnecessary","Introduce the main idea of each paragraph","Should be hidden","Go at the end"]', 'Introduce the main idea of each paragraph', 7),
(540001, 'Formal essay writing avoids:', 'multiple_choice', '["Clear language","Slang and contractions","Evidence","Structure"]', 'Slang and contractions', 8),
(540001, 'Proofreading involves checking for:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","Grammar, spelling, and punctuation errors","Only length","Ideas only"]', 'Grammar, spelling, and punctuation errors', 9),
(540001, 'A well-organized essay follows:', 'multiple_choice', '["No pattern","A logical structure with clear progression","Random order","Stream of consciousness"]', 'A logical structure with clear progression', 10),
(540001, 'Explain the key components of a well-structured essay.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'A well-structured essay includes an introduction with thesis, body paragraphs with evidence, and a conclusion that reinforces the main argument.', 11),

-- GED-LANG Lesson 10: Argumentative Writing (540002)
(540002, 'Argumentative writing aims to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Entertain","Persuade the reader to accept a position","Describe events","Tell a story"]', 'Persuade the reader to accept a position', 1),
(540002, 'A claim in argumentative writing is:', 'multiple_choice', '["A fact","The main argument or position","A question","A summary"]', 'The main argument or position', 2),
(540002, 'Counterarguments should be:', 'multiple_choice', '["Ignored","Acknowledged and refuted","Agreed with entirely","Avoided"]', 'Acknowledged and refuted', 3),
(540002, 'Logical fallacies are:', 'multiple_choice', '["Strong arguments","Errors in reasoning that weaken arguments","Required","Helpful"]', 'Errors in reasoning that weaken arguments', 4),
(540002, 'Ethos appeals to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Emotion","Credibility and ethics","Logic","Fear"]', 'Credibility and ethics', 5),
(540002, 'Pathos appeals to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Logic","Emotions","Authority","Statistics"]', 'Emotions', 6),
(540002, 'Logos appeals to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Emotions","Logic and reason","Personal stories","Credibility"]', 'Logic and reason', 7),
(540002, 'Strong evidence includes:', 'multiple_choice', '["Personal opinions only","Facts, statistics, and expert testimony","Assumptions","Generalizations"]', 'Facts, statistics, and expert testimony', 8),
(540002, 'The call to action in argumentative writing:', 'multiple_choice', '["Is unnecessary","Tells readers what to do or think","Should be vague","Comes first"]', 'Tells readers what to do or think', 9),
(540002, 'Bias in sources should be:', 'multiple_choice', '["Ignored","Identified and evaluated","Preferred","Hidden"]', 'Identified and evaluated', 10),
(540002, 'Describe the elements of effective argumentative writing.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Effective argumentative writing includes a clear claim, strong evidence, acknowledgment of counterarguments, and logical reasoning.', 11),

-- GED-LANG Lesson 11: Reading Comprehension Strategies (540003)
(540003, 'Active reading involves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Passive scanning","Engaging with the text through questioning and annotation","Speed reading only","Memorization"]', 'Engaging with the text through questioning and annotation', 1),
(540003, 'The main idea of a passage is:', 'multiple_choice', '["A minor detail","The central point the author is making","The first sentence always","A supporting fact"]', 'The central point the author is making', 2),
(540003, 'Context clues help readers:', 'multiple_choice', '["Skip words","Determine meaning of unfamiliar words","Ignore vocabulary","Read faster"]', 'Determine meaning of unfamiliar words', 3),
(540003, 'Making inferences means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Guessing randomly","Drawing conclusions from evidence in the text","Copying the text","Ignoring details"]', 'Drawing conclusions from evidence in the text', 4),
(540003, 'Summarizing a passage requires:', 'multiple_choice', '["Copying it exactly","Identifying and restating key points briefly","Adding new information","Ignoring the main idea"]', 'Identifying and restating key points briefly', 5),
(540003, 'Authors purpose can be to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only entertain","Inform, persuade, or entertain","Confuse","None of the above"]', 'Inform, persuade, or entertain', 6),
(540003, 'Tone in writing refers to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Volume","The authors attitude toward the subject","Font size","Length"]', 'The authors attitude toward the subject', 7),
(540003, 'Comparing texts involves finding:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only differences","Similarities and differences","Nothing useful","Errors"]', 'Similarities and differences', 8),
(540003, 'Previewing a text before reading helps:', 'multiple_choice', '["Waste time","Activate prior knowledge and set purpose","Skip reading","Avoid comprehension"]', 'Activate prior knowledge and set purpose', 9),
(540003, 'Asking questions while reading:', 'multiple_choice', '["Slows comprehension","Improves understanding and engagement","Is unnecessary","Should be avoided"]', 'Improves understanding and engagement', 10),
(540003, 'Explain effective strategies for improving reading comprehension.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Effective strategies include previewing, active reading, questioning, making inferences, and summarizing key points.', 11),

-- GED-LANG Lesson 12: Grammar and Sentence Structure (540004)
(540004, 'A complete sentence must have:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only a verb","A subject and a predicate","Only a subject","No punctuation"]', 'A subject and a predicate', 1),
(540004, 'A run-on sentence:', 'multiple_choice', '["Is too short","Joins independent clauses incorrectly","Is always correct","Has no subject"]', 'Joins independent clauses incorrectly', 2),
(540004, 'Subject-verb agreement means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Subjects and verbs can differ","Subjects and verbs must match in number","Verbs are optional","Subjects are optional"]', 'Subjects and verbs must match in number', 3),
(540004, 'A comma splice is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Correct punctuation","Incorrectly joining sentences with only a comma","A type of sentence","Required"]', 'Incorrectly joining sentences with only a comma', 4),
(540004, 'Parallel structure means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random patterns","Using consistent grammatical forms in a series","Mixing forms","Avoiding patterns"]', 'Using consistent grammatical forms in a series', 5),
(540004, 'Pronouns must agree with their:', 'multiple_choice', '["Verbs","Antecedents in number and gender","Adjectives","Nothing"]', 'Antecedents in number and gender', 6),
(540004, 'A fragment is:', 'multiple_choice', '["A complete sentence","An incomplete sentence","A paragraph","A run-on"]', 'An incomplete sentence', 7),
(540004, 'Modifiers should be placed:', 'multiple_choice', '["Anywhere","Near the words they modify","At the end always","At the beginning always"]', 'Near the words they modify', 8),
(540004, 'Active voice is generally preferred because it is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Weaker","More direct and clear","Passive","Confusing"]', 'More direct and clear', 9),
(540004, 'Semicolons are used to:', 'multiple_choice', '["End sentences","Join related independent clauses","Replace periods always","Introduce lists"]', 'Join related independent clauses', 10),
(540004, 'Explain common grammar errors and how to correct them.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Common errors include run-ons, fragments, subject-verb disagreement, and comma splices, corrected by proper punctuation and structure.', 11),

-- GED-LANG Lesson 13: Vocabulary Development (540005)
(540005, 'Root words help readers:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","Understand word meanings and build vocabulary","Confuse words","Avoid learning"]', 'Understand word meanings and build vocabulary', 1),
(540005, 'Prefixes are added to:', 'multiple_choice', '["The end of words","The beginning of words","The middle","Nowhere"]', 'The beginning of words', 2),
(540005, 'Suffixes change a words:', 'multiple_choice', '["Spelling only","Meaning or part of speech","Nothing","Pronunciation only"]', 'Meaning or part of speech', 3),
(540005, 'The prefix un- means:', 'multiple_choice', '["More","Not or opposite","Again","Before"]', 'Not or opposite', 4),
(540005, 'Synonyms are words that:', 'multiple_choice', '["Mean the opposite","Have similar meanings","Sound alike","Are spelled alike"]', 'Have similar meanings', 5),
(540005, 'Antonyms are words that:', 'multiple_choice', '["Mean the same","Have opposite meanings","Sound alike","Are spelled alike"]', 'Have opposite meanings', 6),
(540005, 'Context clues include:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing helpful","Definitions, examples, and surrounding words","Random information","Pictures only"]', 'Definitions, examples, and surrounding words', 7),
(540005, 'Denotation refers to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Emotional meaning","Dictionary definition","Personal interpretation","Slang meaning"]', 'Dictionary definition', 8),
(540005, 'Connotation refers to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Dictionary definition","Emotional or cultural associations","Spelling","Pronunciation"]', 'Emotional or cultural associations', 9),
(540005, 'Building vocabulary requires:', 'multiple_choice', '["No effort","Regular reading and practice","Avoiding new words","Memorization only"]', 'Regular reading and practice', 10),
(540005, 'Describe strategies for building vocabulary effectively.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Effective strategies include learning roots, prefixes, suffixes, using context clues, and regular reading practice.', 11),

-- GED-LANG Lesson 14: Analyzing Literary Texts (540006)
(540006, 'Theme in literature is:', 'multiple_choice', '["The plot","The underlying message or central idea","The setting","The characters"]', 'The underlying message or central idea', 1),
(540006, 'Characterization reveals:', 'multiple_choice', '["Setting only","Personality traits and motivations of characters","Plot only","Theme only"]', 'Personality traits and motivations of characters', 2),
(540006, 'Symbolism uses objects to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Decorate","Represent abstract ideas or concepts","Confuse readers","Fill space"]', 'Represent abstract ideas or concepts', 3),
(540006, 'Foreshadowing provides:', 'multiple_choice', '["Confusion","Hints about future events","Flashbacks","Character descriptions"]', 'Hints about future events', 4),
(540006, 'Point of view affects:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","How the story is told and what readers know","Only setting","Only theme"]', 'How the story is told and what readers know', 5),
(540006, 'First-person narration uses:', 'multiple_choice', '["He/she","I/we","They","You"]', 'I/we', 6),
(540006, 'Conflict in literature can be:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only external","Internal or external","Neither","Avoided"]', 'Internal or external', 7),
(540006, 'Imagery appeals to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Logic only","The five senses","Nothing","Statistics"]', 'The five senses', 8),
(540006, 'Irony occurs when:', 'multiple_choice', '["Things are as expected","There is a contrast between expectation and reality","Nothing happens","Characters agree"]', 'There is a contrast between expectation and reality', 9),
(540006, 'Analyzing literature requires:', 'multiple_choice', '["Surface reading only","Close reading and critical thinking","Skimming","Memorization"]', 'Close reading and critical thinking', 10),
(540006, 'Explain key literary elements and how to analyze them in texts.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Key elements include theme, characterization, symbolism, point of view, and conflict, analyzed through close reading.', 11),

-- GED-LANG Lesson 15: Analyzing Informational Texts (540007)
(540007, 'Informational texts aim to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Entertain only","Inform, explain, or persuade","Tell fictional stories","Avoid facts"]', 'Inform, explain, or persuade', 1),
(540007, 'The central idea is:', 'multiple_choice', '["A minor detail","The main point of the text","The authors name","The title"]', 'The main point of the text', 2),
(540007, 'Supporting details:', 'multiple_choice', '["Contradict the main idea","Provide evidence for the central idea","Are unnecessary","Should be ignored"]', 'Provide evidence for the central idea', 3),
(540007, 'Text structure includes:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random organization","Cause/effect, compare/contrast, sequence","No pattern","Only chronological"]', 'Cause/effect, compare/contrast, sequence', 4),
(540007, 'Evaluating sources requires checking:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","Credibility, bias, and accuracy","Only length","Only date"]', 'Credibility, bias, and accuracy', 5),
(540007, 'Facts can be:', 'multiple_choice', '["Never verified","Proven true or false","Only opinions","Ignored"]', 'Proven true or false', 6),
(540007, 'Opinions express:', 'multiple_choice', '["Verifiable facts","Personal beliefs or judgments","Universal truths","Nothing"]', 'Personal beliefs or judgments', 7),
(540007, 'Authors bias may:', 'multiple_choice', '["Not exist","Influence how information is presented","Be obvious always","Be helpful always"]', 'Influence how information is presented', 8),
(540007, 'Synthesizing information means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Copying","Combining information from multiple sources","Ignoring sources","Summarizing one source"]', 'Combining information from multiple sources', 9),
(540007, 'Critical reading involves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Accepting everything","Questioning and evaluating the text","Speed reading","Skimming"]', 'Questioning and evaluating the text', 10),
(540007, 'Describe how to analyze and evaluate informational texts effectively.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Effective analysis includes identifying central ideas, evaluating evidence, recognizing bias, and synthesizing information.', 11),

-- GED-LANG Lesson 16: Extended Response Writing (540008)
(540008, 'The GED extended response requires:', 'multiple_choice', '["A creative story","An evidence-based argument analyzing source texts","A personal narrative","A summary only"]', 'An evidence-based argument analyzing source texts', 1),
(540008, 'Time management for the extended response should include:', 'multiple_choice', '["Writing immediately","Planning, writing, and revising time","Only writing","Only planning"]', 'Planning, writing, and revising time', 2),
(540008, 'Evidence from source texts should be:', 'multiple_choice', '["Ignored","Quoted or paraphrased with analysis","Made up","Copied entirely"]', 'Quoted or paraphrased with analysis', 3),
(540008, 'The response should analyze:', 'multiple_choice', '["Personal experiences only","How well the author supports their argument","Random topics","Unrelated texts"]', 'How well the author supports their argument', 4),
(540008, 'A strong thesis for the extended response:', 'multiple_choice', '["Is vague","Takes a clear position on the effectiveness of the argument","Avoids the topic","Is a question"]', 'Takes a clear position on the effectiveness of the argument', 5),
(540008, 'Body paragraphs should include:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only opinions","Topic sentences, evidence, and analysis","Random thoughts","No structure"]', 'Topic sentences, evidence, and analysis', 6),
(540008, 'Transitions help:', 'multiple_choice', '["Confuse readers","Connect ideas and improve flow","Add length only","Nothing"]', 'Connect ideas and improve flow', 7),
(540008, 'The conclusion should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Introduce new ideas","Reinforce the thesis and summarize key points","Be skipped","Repeat the introduction exactly"]', 'Reinforce the thesis and summarize key points', 8),
(540008, 'Revision should check for:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","Clarity, organization, and grammar","Length only","Spelling only"]', 'Clarity, organization, and grammar', 9),
(540008, 'Scoring criteria include:', 'multiple_choice', '["Length only","Analysis, organization, language use, and conventions","Handwriting","Speed"]', 'Analysis, organization, language use, and conventions', 10),
(540008, 'Explain the key components of a successful GED extended response.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'A successful response includes a clear thesis, evidence-based analysis, logical organization, and proper conventions.', 11),

-- GED-LANG Lesson 17: Test-Taking Strategies (540009)
(540009, 'Before the test, you should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Stay up all night","Get adequate rest and prepare materials","Skip breakfast","Arrive late"]', 'Get adequate rest and prepare materials', 1),
(540009, 'Reading questions carefully helps:', 'multiple_choice', '["Waste time","Understand what is being asked","Nothing","Confuse you"]', 'Understand what is being asked', 2),
(540009, 'Process of elimination involves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Guessing randomly","Removing obviously wrong answers","Choosing the first answer","Skipping questions"]', 'Removing obviously wrong answers', 3),
(540009, 'Time management during the test means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Spending all time on one question","Pacing yourself through all sections","Rushing through everything","Ignoring time"]', 'Pacing yourself through all sections', 4),
(540009, 'If unsure of an answer:', 'multiple_choice', '["Leave it blank","Make an educated guess and move on","Spend unlimited time","Give up"]', 'Make an educated guess and move on', 5),
(540009, 'Reviewing answers is important to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Waste time","Catch and correct errors","Change everything","Nothing"]', 'Catch and correct errors', 6),
(540009, 'Test anxiety can be managed by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Ignoring it","Deep breathing and positive self-talk","Panicking","Leaving the test"]', 'Deep breathing and positive self-talk', 7),
(540009, 'Key words in questions like NOT or EXCEPT indicate:', 'multiple_choice', '["Normal questions","Looking for the opposite or exception","Trick questions","Easy questions"]', 'Looking for the opposite or exception', 8),
(540009, 'For reading passages, you should:', 'multiple_choice', '["Skip the passage","Read actively and refer back for answers","Memorize everything","Guess without reading"]', 'Read actively and refer back for answers', 9),
(540009, 'Staying calm during the test helps:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","Maintain focus and think clearly","Waste time","Increase anxiety"]', 'Maintain focus and think clearly', 10),
(540009, 'Describe effective test-taking strategies for the GED Language Arts test.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Effective strategies include preparation, time management, process of elimination, careful reading, and managing test anxiety.', 11),

-- GED-LANG Lesson 18: Practice and Review (540010)
(540010, 'Regular practice helps:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","Build skills and confidence","Increase anxiety","Waste time"]', 'Build skills and confidence', 1),
(540010, 'Reviewing mistakes helps:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","Identify areas for improvement","Feel bad","Repeat errors"]', 'Identify areas for improvement', 2),
(540010, 'Practice tests simulate:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing useful","Actual test conditions and format","Easy questions only","Irrelevant content"]', 'Actual test conditions and format', 3),
(540010, 'Weak areas should be:', 'multiple_choice', '["Ignored","Given extra study time and practice","Avoided","Accepted"]', 'Given extra study time and practice', 4),
(540010, 'Study schedules help:', 'multiple_choice', '["Waste time","Organize and prioritize learning","Nothing","Increase stress"]', 'Organize and prioritize learning', 5),
(540010, 'Active recall involves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Passive reading","Testing yourself on material","Highlighting only","Copying notes"]', 'Testing yourself on material', 6),
(540010, 'Spaced repetition means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Cramming","Reviewing material at increasing intervals","Studying once","Forgetting"]', 'Reviewing material at increasing intervals', 7),
(540010, 'Study groups can help:', 'multiple_choice', '["Distract","Share knowledge and stay motivated","Waste time","Nothing"]', 'Share knowledge and stay motivated', 8),
(540010, 'Using multiple resources:', 'multiple_choice', '["Is confusing","Provides different perspectives and reinforcement","Is unnecessary","Should be avoided"]', 'Provides different perspectives and reinforcement', 9),
(540010, 'Confidence comes from:', 'multiple_choice', '["Luck","Preparation and practice","Avoiding study","Guessing"]', 'Preparation and practice', 10),
(540010, 'Explain effective study and review strategies for GED preparation.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Effective strategies include regular practice, reviewing mistakes, using practice tests, and focusing on weak areas.', 11);
